subject: A Review of Set Theory
question: Find a simpler expression of $[(A \cup B) \cap (A \cup C) \cap (B^c \cap C^c)]$ assuming all three sets $A, B, C$ intersect.
explanation: Using a Venn diagram one can easily see that $[(A \cup B) \cap (A \cup C) \cap (B^c \cap C^c)] =$ If $A \cap B = \emptyset$ we say that A and B are disjoint sets.
